justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth. Formal escalation is produced with explicit call for executive arbitration. No acceptance criteria are produced — the agent explicitly refuses to define them until the conflict is resolved. confidence.level is 40, within the <=45 threshold. The proposed compromise (Option C: zone-based/ETA tracking) is clearly labeled as a recommendation requiring executive approval, not presented as a resolution.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the conflict as mutually exclusive and unresolvable at the feature level
Both stakeholders' underlying needs are reframed thoughtfully — Customer Success needs predictability, Operations needs dignity — without dismissing either
Explicit refusal to author acceptance criteria against a contradictory spec, with clear reasoning
Identified a missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) and flagged it as a blocking dependency — goes beyond the rubric expectations
Escalation is formal, structured, and directed at executive leadership with concrete options
Compromise options are clearly labeled as proposals requiring authorization, not resolutions
Business impact analysis is balanced and quantified where possible (courier recruiting costs, support call percentages)
Edge cases are identified but explicitly noted as undefinable until the conflict is resolved
rejection_reasons section reinforces that neither extreme can be implemented, preventing downstream teams from cherry-picking one side

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements — Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing at all. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by technical design alone; it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Orde

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'missing_context'
